root_text.sn51.31:1.3: katamesaṁ dhammānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā moggallāno bhikkhu evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo”ti?
root_text.sn51.31:2.2: “catunnaṁ kho, bhikkhave, iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā moggallāno bhikkhu evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo.
root_text.sn51.31:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, moggallāno bhikkhu chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti—
root_text.sn51.31:3.3: iti me chando na ca atilīno bhavissati, na ca atippaggahito bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhitto bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhitto bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.31:3.5: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure;
root_text.sn51.31:3.6: yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho;
root_text.sn51.31:3.7: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā.
root_text.sn51.31:3.8: Iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.31:3.12: iti me vīmaṁsā na ca atilīnā bhavissati, na ca atippaggahitā bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittā bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhittā bhavissati …pe…
root_text.sn51.31:3.13: iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.31:3.14: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā moggallāno bhikkhu evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo.
root_text.sn51.31:4.1: Imesañca pana, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā moggallāno bhikkhu evaṁ anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhoti—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hoti, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hoti …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteti.
root_text.sn51.31:4.2: Imesañca pana, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā moggallāno bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.

translation_text.sn51.31:1.1: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn51.31:1.3: What things has the mendicant Moggallāna developed and cultivated so as to have such power and might?”
translation_text.sn51.31:2.1: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. He is our guide and our refuge. …”
translation_text.sn51.31:2.2: “The mendicant Moggallāna has become so powerful and mighty by developing and cultivating the four bases of psychic power.
translation_text.sn51.31:3.2: Moggallāna develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.31:3.3: He thinks: ‘My enthusiasm won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.’
translation_text.sn51.31:3.4: And he meditates perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.31:3.5: as before, so behind; as behind, so before;
translation_text.sn51.31:3.6: as below, so above; as above, so below;
translation_text.sn51.31:3.7: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.31:3.8: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, he develops a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.31:3.9: He develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy …
translation_text.sn51.31:3.12: He thinks: ‘My inquiry won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.’ …
translation_text.sn51.31:3.13: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, he develops a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.31:3.14: The mendicant Moggallāna has become so powerful and mighty by developing and cultivating these four bases of psychic power.
translation_text.sn51.31:4.1: And by developing and cultivating these four bases of psychic power, the mendicant Moggallāna wields the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying himself and becoming one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn51.31:4.2: And by developing and cultivating these four bases of psychic power, the mendicant Moggallāna realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And he lives having realized it with his own insight due to the ending of defilements.”
